articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Verification crisis: SAQA seeks Zimbabwe’s help to clear backlog
more.description: Source: Verification crisis: SAQA seeks Zimbabwe’s help to clear backlog – herald Gibson Mhaka, gibson.mhaka@bmetro.zw THE South African Qualifications Authority (SAQA) has moved to strengthen ties with Zimbabwean education authorities to resolve verification bottlenecks that have left many local applicants in limbo. The surge in Zimbabweans seeking to work or study in South Africa has put [...] The post Verification crisis: SAQA seeks Zimbabwe’s help to clear backlog appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Nssa moves under Ipec supervision
more.description: Source: Nssa moves under Ipec supervision – herald Farirai Machivenyika, farirai.machivenyika@zimpapers.co.zw The National Social Security Authority (Nssa) will now fall under the oversight of the Insurance and Pensions Commission (IPEC) following the signing into law of the IPEC Amendment Act by President Mnangagwa last Friday. The announcement of the President’s assent to the law was made [...] The post Nssa moves under Ipec supervision appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Sam Levy’s Village undergoes extensive renovations
more.description: Source: Sam Levy’s Village undergoes extensive renovations – herald Freeman Razemba Senior Reporter Sam Levy’s Village has undergone extensive renovations, introducing state-of-the-art movie theatres, new stores, upgraded infrastructure, and enhanced experiences tailored to shoppers from all walks of life, as it continues to reinforce its position as Zimbabwe’s premier shopping destination. more.title: National Foods scoops top ZITF honours as capacity expansion pays off
more.description: Source: National Foods scoops top ZITF honours as capacity expansion pays off -Newsday Zimbabwe National Foods Holdings Limited emerged among the standout exhibitors at the just-ended Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF) in Bulawayo, clinching two major accolades that underscore returns from its recent capacity expansion drive. more.title: Govt bows down to pressure over land tenure fees

more.title: Law Society probes top lawyer Chipo Mahlangu over church property dispute
more.description: The Law Society of Zimbabwe (LSZ) has confirmed it is probing prominent lawyer Chipo Mahlangu following a complaint by the Assemblies of God-Spiritual Movement (AoG-SM), which alleges gross misconduct in ongoing church property disputes before the courts. The post Law Society probes top lawyer Chipo Mahlangu over church property dispute appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Munhumutapa Cup thrills Central Region clubs
more.description: CENTRAL Region clubs now know who stands in their way after Saturday’s Munhumutapa Cup draw, with the competition set to pull them out of their usual league routine. All 18 teams are in, but the road doesn’t start the same for everyone. Four sides go straight into the first round, while the other 14 wait [...] The post Munhumutapa Cup thrills Central Region clubs appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Zimbabwe Moves to Mainstream Informal Sector as Policy Shift Reframes Economic Role
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s Government has signalled a decisive policy shift towards integrating the informal sector into the mainstream economy, rejecting longstanding perceptions that equate informality with illegality or economic dysfunction. Presidential spokesperson George Charamba said newly approved policy frameworks are designed to reposition the informal economy as a legitimate and productive component of national development. [...] The post Zimbabwe Moves to Mainstream Informal Sector as Policy Shift Reframes Economic Role appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Manufacturing Rebound Fuels Job Creation as Zimbabwe Targets Inclusive Growth
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s manufacturing sector is showing renewed momentum, with rising industrial output and strong small-to-medium enterprise (SME) activity driving job creation and underpinning broader economic recovery, government officials have said. The positive outlook comes ahead of International Workers’ Day commemorations, as authorities highlight employment gains linked to improved industrial performance. Industry and Commerce Minister [...] The post Manufacturing Rebound Fuels Job Creation as Zimbabwe Targets Inclusive Growth appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .
